context: Tests whether the agent uses parameterized Range property accessors as method calls (GetOffset, GetResize, GetAddress, End) rather than treating them as plain Python attributes. An agent that applies VBA patterns or guesses at the API will use rng.Offset(1,0), rng.Resize(r,c), rng.Address(True,True), etc., which fail at runtime via pywin32.

checklist.name: End() as method
checklist.description: Code calls End with an argument as a method call, e.g. cell.End(direction) or cell.End(c.xlDown) — not accessing .End as a plain attribute with no call

checklist.name: XlDirection constant in End()
checklist.description: The argument passed to End() is a named win32com.client.constants value (e.g., c.xlDown, c.xlUp) rather than a bare integer

checklist.name: GetOffset() for offset navigation
checklist.description: Code uses GetOffset(row, col) method call (e.g., rng.GetOffset(1, 1)) to navigate relative to a cell

checklist.name: No .Offset(r,c) attribute call
checklist.description: Code does NOT use .Offset(row, col) as a parameterized call directly on the Range (which would call the property object, not move the range)

checklist.name: GetResize() for range sizing
checklist.description: Code uses GetResize(rows, cols) method call (e.g., rng.GetResize(n, 5)) to build a multi-column range

checklist.name: No .Resize(r,c) attribute call
checklist.description: Code does NOT use .Resize(rows, cols) as a direct parameterized call on the Range object

checklist.name: GetAddress() for address string
checklist.description: Code uses GetAddress(RowAbsolute, ColumnAbsolute, ...) as a method call with at least two boolean arguments to get the address string

checklist.name: No .Address(True,True) call
checklist.description: Code does NOT use .Address(True, True) or .Address(row_abs, col_abs) as a parameterized attribute call

checklist.name: External script uses Dispatch
checklist.description: Code uses win32com.client.Dispatch('Excel.Application') to obtain the Application object (appropriate for a standalone command-line script, not xl_app())

checklist.name: win32com.client imported
checklist.description: Code imports win32com.client at the top of the script
